title: Split the Extension
description: Import the os module. Create a variable called file_path with the value 'data.txt'. Use **os.path.splitext()** to split it, and print just the extension part (the second element of the returned tuple). Expected output: .txt
starter_code:
# Import the os module
# Create file_path = 'data.txt'
# Print just the extension using os.path.splitext()

code_requirements.fail_message: Import the os module with 'import os'.

code_requirements.description: Uses os.path.splitext() to split the file extension
code_requirements.fail_message: Use os.path.splitext() to get the extension instead of hardcoding '.txt'.

hints: Call os.path.splitext(file_path) — it returns a tuple like ('data', '.txt'). Get the second element with [1] and print it.
